[TITLE]: Shock weather warning: WORST Atlantic storm of the winter is now 'ROARING' towards Britain | Nature | News | Express.co.uk
[METADATA/INTRO]: Monster 75 FOOT wave smashes into BRITISH coast: Atlantic superstorm to hit UK TOMORROW BRITAIN has been battered by the biggest wave in HISTORY - a staggering 75ft monster - just hours before a huge Atlantic superstorm is set to...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Resident Robert Parker said: “It was like an earthquake. 
 “I’ve been in some terrible storms in the North Sea but last night was just a force of nature.” 
 Another local, Jeff Deacon, added: “This is surreal. I’ve never seen anything like this. There’s debris all over the road – it’s like a war zone.” 
 A 100ft stretch of seawall in the town collapsed into the swirling waters, leaving the railway line hanging in mid-air. 
 Dawlish, Devon in its normally tranquil state 
 ...and yesterday, after the sea wall and railway collapsed 
 The damage sparked chaos for travellers as First Great Western were forced to halt the busy Penzance-to-Exeter service.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2015-02-24
[TITLE]: UK weather: Dramatic pictures show huge 'supertides' battering the Cornish coast - The Mirror
[METADATA/INTRO]: UK weather: Dramatic pictures show huge 'supertides' battering the Cornish coast High seas crashed into coastal regions and northern areas as stormy weather struck parts of the UK...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: UK weather: Dramatic pictures show huge 'supertides' battering the Cornish coast 
 High seas crashed into coastal regions and northern areas as stormy weather struck parts of the UK 
 SupertidesThese dramatic pictures show huge waves battering the Cornish coast as supertides hit the UK. 
 The swell batters the sea defences at Sennon Cove as coastal regions and northern areas were hit by stormy weather and icy conditions. 
 Flood warnings were issued for Wales and parts of the north and south west England, with wave swells of up to 50ft as Britain and Ireland have seen some of the highest tides in decades.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Photograph of waves crashing ashore near village wasn't taken during Storm Eunice in Feb 2022
[METADATA/INTRO]: Summary Photograph of waves crashing ashore near village wasn't taken during Storm Eunice in Feb 2022 (Reuters) - A photograph of waves crashing ashore in a coastal town was not captured in February 2022 and likely originated as satire, although...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: One user shared the photograph on Facebook and said: “Wow!!! Scary stuff!! Just heard that Blaenau Ffestiniog is getting battered by waves as well”. Blaenau Ffestiniog is also many miles inland. 
 Another said: “Yesterday’s storm must have been the strongest storm on record”. 
 The photograph is not recent, however, and has been online since at least 2014. 
 The image features in a guide blog about Cornwall, England, published in February 2014. 
 The town pictured is Sennen in Cornwall, England. Other photographs of waves crashing by the town can be found on the photograph agency, Alamy. 
 A similar image of waves crashing by Sennan, Cornwall, was published in The Mirror in February 2015.
